responses.rationale: The simulated proof attempt cannot establish that all verification-affecting attacker-controlled fields are included in the cache key.

finding.title: Verification cache key may omit attacker-controlled input
finding.summary: The proof attempt cannot establish that all attacker-controlled fields are included in the cache key.

proofAttempt.prove: The implementation must show that every verification-affecting attacker-controlled field is included in the key material.
proofAttempt.stressTest: Counterexample candidate: two verification requests differ in a verification-affecting attacker-controlled field but map to the same cache key.
proofAttempt.report: Treat as a candidate until reviewed by the three-gate security review.

responses.rationale: The simulated proof attempt also found a fixture helper that resembles key construction but is expected to be classified out-of-scope by the review stage.

finding.title: Test-only cache helper resembles production key construction
finding.summary: The proof attempt found similar test helper code, but the path may be outside the audit scope.

affectedLocations.reason: Simulated fixture response for a test-only helper path.

proofAttempt.map: Candidate code path appears only in fixture helper files.
proofAttempt.prove: The implementation would need to show the helper is reachable from production entry points.
proofAttempt.stressTest: No production entry point reaches the fixture helper in the MVP scope.
proofAttempt.report: Candidate is expected to be rejected by the scope gate.
